title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks detects linkage disequilibrium (LD) blocks genome-wide SNP data extends pipeline haplotype reconstruction, diversity analysis, genomic prediction feature engineering. Two LD metrics available: Standard r² – fast, kinship correction, suitable 10 M+ markers unstructured populations. Kinship-adjusted rV² – corrects relatedness-induced LD inflation, livestock, inbred lines, family-based cohorts. See LD Metrics vignette statistical detail decision table. C++/Armadillo computational core makes algorithm approximately 40x faster original Big-LD implementation (compiled code) typical window sizes. vignette covers complete pipeline – reading genotype data, block detection, haplotype analysis, parameter tuning – using ldx_geno example dataset (120 individuals, 230 SNPs, 3 chromosomes, 9 simulated LD blocks).

what: 2. Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks built clique-based segmentation algorithm Kim et al. (2018), introduced interval graph modelling LD bins principled alternative sliding-window approaches. mathematical core – CLQD bin assignment, maximum-weight independent set block construction, Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration – preserved exactly. LDxBlocks extends foundation address three limitations original implementation become critical modern breeding genomics programmes.

previous_headings: 2. Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big_LD() calls cor() inside boundary-scan loop per CLQD() call, matrix operations running R interpreter. 50,000-SNP chromosome default leng = 200 amounts approximately 150,000 small R-level matrix multiplications per chromosome. WGS rice panel 3 million SNPs across 12 chromosomes, original implementation take days workstation. LDxBlocks replaces critical paths seven compiled C++ functions: outer loop compute_r2_cpp() parallelised OpenMP. 1,500-SNP window 500 individuals, r² matrix computes milliseconds rather seconds.
code: # These calls are now C++/Armadillo + OpenMP -- not R loops compute_r2_cpp(geno, digits = -1L, n_threads = 8L)   # ~40x faster maf_filter_cpp(geno, maf_cut = 0.05)                  # ~10x faster boundary_scan_cpp(geno, start, end, half_w, threshold) # ~20x faster

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big_LD() requires complete genotype matrix RAM detection begins. 10M-marker, 5,000-individual WGS dataset approximately 400 GB R double matrix – impossible workstation. LDxBlocks enforces strict never-full-genome memory model. six supported formats stream genotypes one chromosome window time:
code: # VCF auto-converts to GDS cache; detection streams one window at a time be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.vcf.gz") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", n_threads = 8L) # Peak RAM ~ n_samples x subSegmSize x 8 bytes = 60 MB for n=5000, w=1500

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big-LD stops block table. breeding programmes immediate next questions : haplotypes exist? diverse ? blocks harbour QTLs? build multi-locus genomic prediction model? LDxBlocks provides complete answer:
code: # From blocks to genomic prediction features in four lines haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps) qtl  <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_results, blocks, be$snp_info) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5, encoding = "additive_012")

what: 2.4 What is preserved from the original
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: following components unchanged Kim et al. (2018): CLQD() (internal): bin vector assignment via maximal clique enumeration greedy density/maximal priority selection constructLDblock() (internal): maximum-weight independent set via dynamic programming sorted interval sequences CLQmode, clstgap, split logic Block table column format (start, end, start.rsID, end.rsID, start.bp, end.bp) – drop-compatible tools accept original Big-LD output

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: read_geno() auto-detects file format extension returns LDxBlocks_backend object. Six formats supported:
code: be_vcf <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz") be_csv <- read_geno("mydata.csv") be_hmp <- read_geno("mydata.hmp.txt") be_gds <- read_geno("mydata.gds")    # SNPRelate GDS; streams SNP windows be_bed <- read_geno("mydata.bed")    # PLINK BED; memory-mapped be <- read_geno(ldx_geno, format = "matrix", snp_info = ldx_snp_info) be #> LDxBlocks backend #>   Type      : matrix  #>   Samples   : 120  #>   SNPs      : 230  #>   Chr       : 1, 2, 3 be$n_samples #> [1] 120 be$n_snps #> [1] 230 be$sample_ids[1:4] #> [1] "ind001" "ind002" "ind003" "ind004" head(be$snp_info) #>      SNP CHR  POS REF ALT #> 1 rs1001   1 1000   G   C #> 2 rs1002   1 2134   C   G #> 3 rs1003   1 3089   T   A #> 4 rs1004   1 4067   A   G #> 5 rs1005   1 5246   G   A #> 6 rs1006   1 6312   C   T chunk <- read_chunk(be, 1:30) dim(chunk) #> [1] 120  30 close_backend(be)

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: blues argument accepts pre-adjusted phenotype means mixed model. format flexible — four structures accepted: Format 1: Named numeric vector (single trait, simplest) Format 2: Data frame, single trait common format reading output ASReml-R, lme4, SpATS. column names accepted — just tell function columns use: Format 3: Data frame, multiple traits blue_cols = NULL (default), numeric non-ID columns used automatically. Format 4: Named list (different individuals per trait) Column requirements ID matching package ships ldx_blues inst/extdata/example_blues.csv showing expected format (columns: id, YLD, RES): common source errors ID mismatch phenotype file genotype matrix. Verify :
code: blues <- c(ind001 = 4.21, ind002 = 3.87, ind003 = 5.14) # names = individual IDs matching rownames(geno_matrix) blues <- read.csv("blues.csv")  # e.g. columns: "Genotype", "YLD_BLUE" res   <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                    blues    = blues,                                    id_col   = "Genotype",   # ID column name                                    blue_col = "YLD_BLUE")   # BLUE column name blues_mt <- read.csv("blues_mt.csv")  # columns: id, YLD, DIS, PHT res_mt   <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                       blues           = blues_mt,                                       id_col          = "id",                                       blue_cols       = c("YLD", "DIS", "PHT"),                                       importance_rule = "any") blues <- list(   YLD = c(ind001 = 4.21, ind002 = 3.87),   DIS = c(ind001 = 0.32, ind003 = 0.28)  # ind002 missing, ind003 added ) blues_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_blues.csv", package = "LDxBlocks") blues      <- read.csv(blues_file) head(blues, 3) #>       id     YLD     RES #> 1 ind001  0.8909 -0.8208 #> 2 ind002 -0.8711  0.9927 #> 3 ind003 -0.9154 -0.9956 data(ldx_geno) all(blues$id %in% rownames(ldx_geno))  # should be TRUE

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: Use chr parameter run_Big_LD_all_chr() restrict detection one specific chromosomes. uses streaming memory model produces output columns genome-wide run.
code: blocks_chr1 <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   method      = "r2",   CLQcut      = 0.55,   leng        = 15L,   subSegmSize = 100L,   chr         = "1",         # restrict to chromosome 1 only   n_threads   = 1L,   verbose     = FALSE ) nrow(blocks_chr1) #> [1] 3 unique(blocks_chr1$CHR)      # confirms only chr 1 processed #> [1] "1"

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: WGS-density panels (> 500k SNPs), default CLQmode = "Density" (Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration) can suffer exponential blowup — single 1500-SNP window dense LD can stall hours. Two parameters fix :
code: # Recommended for WGS panels (> 500 k SNPs) blocks_wgs <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",    # polynomial O(n log n) — guaranteed connected   max_bp_distance = 500000L,     # skip r² for pairs > 500 kb (near-O(p))   CLQcut          = 0.70,        # sparser LD graph   subSegmSize     = 500L,        # smaller windows   leng            = 50L,         # narrower boundary scan at WGS density   checkLargest    = TRUE,        # belt-and-suspenders guard   n_threads       = n_threads )

text: extract_haplotypes() accepts either numeric dosage matrix (0/1/2/NA) phased list read_phased_vcf() / phase_with_pedigree(). unphased mode individual receives one diploid allele string per block:

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: WGS data phased externally (Beagle, SHAPEIT, pedigree), load phased VCF first pass resulting list extract_haplotypes(). Phased mode detected automatically hap1/hap2 structure:
code: # Statistical phasing via Beagle 5.x phase_with_beagle(input_vcf = "raw.vcf.gz", out_prefix = "phased",                   nthreads = 8L) phased <- read_phased_vcf("phased.vcf.gz", min_maf = 0.05)  # Pedigree-based phasing ped    <- data.frame(id = c("off1","sire1","dam1"),                      sire = c("sire1", NA, NA),                      dam  = c("dam1",  NA, NA)) phased <- phase_with_pedigree(ldx_geno, pedigree = ped)  # Extract haplotypes — phased strings: "011|100" haps_p <- extract_haplotypes(phased, ldx_snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) head(haps_p[[1]])   # "011|100" format

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: GWAS results available, map significant markers onto LD blocks define QTL regions flag pleiotropic blocks:
code: # ldx_gwas has a `trait` column (TraitA / TraitB) enabling pleiotropic detection. # For single-trait GWAS simply omit trait_col — the function still works, # setting n_traits = 1 and pleiotropic = FALSE for all blocks. qtl <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_results = ldx_gwas,   blocks       = blocks,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   p_threshold  = 5e-8,   trait_col    = "trait"      # omit this argument for single-trait GWAS ) head(qtl[, c("block_id","CHR","n_snps_block","n_sig_markers",              "lead_snp","traits","pleiotropic")]) #>                block_id CHR n_snps_block n_sig_markers lead_snp traits #> 1 block_1_156776_181114   1           25             1   rs1070 TraitA #>   pleiotropic #> 1       FALSE subset(qtl, pleiotropic)      # blocks with hits from both TraitA and TraitB #>  [1] block_id      CHR           start_bp      end_bp        n_snps_block  #>  [6] n_sig_markers lead_snp      lead_p        lead_beta     sig_snps      #> [11] sig_betas     traits        n_traits      pleiotropic   #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

what: 11. Step 8 — Feature matrix for genomic prediction
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: build_haplotype_feature_matrix() supports two encoding schemes phased unphased input: Write feature matrix:
code: # Additive 0/1/2 (phased) or 0/2 (unphased) — recommended for GBLUP/rrBLUP feat_add <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes = haps,   top_n      = 5L,   encoding   = "additive_012",   scale_features = TRUE ) dim(feat_add) #> [1] 120  45  # Presence/absence 0/1 — for kernel methods or random forest feat_pa <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes = haps,   top_n      = 5L,   encoding   = "presence_01" ) dim(feat_pa) #> [1] 120  45 # Numeric: rows=haplotypes, cols=individuals, values=0/1/2/NA dosage write_haplotype_numeric(feat_add, "hap_matrix_numeric.csv",                          haplotypes = haps, snp_info = ldx_snp_info)  # Character: nucleotide sequence per individual per haplotype allele write_haplotype_character(haplotypes = haps, snp_info = ldx_snp_info,                            out_file = "hap_matrix_character.txt")

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: pre-adjusted phenotype values (BLUEs adjusted entry means) available, full Tong et al. (2025) haplotype stacking pipeline runs single call. blues accepts four formats described Step 2 (named numeric vector, single-trait data frame, multi-trait data frame, named list). See Step 2 column name requirements:
code: blues <- read.csv("blues.csv")  # columns: id, YLD pred  <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   blues       = blues,   id_col      = "id",   blue_col    = "YLD" ) pred$block_importance[pred$block_importance$important, ] sort(pred$gebv, decreasing = TRUE)  # ranked GEBVs  # Integrate GWAS + variance + diversity evidence priority <- rank_haplotype_blocks(   diversity   = pred$diversity,   qtl_regions = qtl,          # from define_qtl_regions()   pred_result = pred ) priority$ranked_blocks[priority$ranked_blocks$rank_score >= 0.9, ]

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: Pass data frame multiple trait columns — named list named numeric vectors — run_haplotype_prediction() analyse traits simultaneously single shared GRM. traits fitted via rrBLUP::kin.blup() per trait using shared GRM, making cross-trait block importance values directly comparable. Block importance aggregated across traits rankings specific single trait:
code: blues_df <- read.csv("blues.csv")  # columns: id, YLD, DIS, PHT res_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix     = ldx_geno,   snp_info        = ldx_snp_info,   blocks          = blocks,   blues           = blues_df,   id_col          = "id",   blue_cols       = c("YLD", "DIS", "PHT"),   importance_rule = "any"   # flag block if important for >= 1 trait ) res_mt$solver_used   # 'rrBLUP' # Cross-trait block importance res_mt$block_importance[res_mt$block_importance$important_any,    c("block_id", "var_scaled_YLD", "var_scaled_DIS",     "var_scaled_mean", "n_traits_important")]

code: grid <- expand.grid(   CLQcut = c(0.45, 0.55, 0.65), clstgap = 1e5, leng = 15,   subSegmSize = 100, split = FALSE, checkLargest = FALSE,   MAFcut = 0.05, CLQmode = "Density", kin_method = "chol",   digits = -1, appendrare = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  result <- tune_LD_params(   geno_matrix    = ldx_geno,   snp_info       = ldx_snp_info,   gwas_df        = ldx_gwas,   grid           = grid,   prefer_perfect = TRUE,   target_bp_band = c(5e4, 5e5),   seed           = 42L ) result$best_params #> $CLQcut #> [1] 0.45 #>  #> $min_freq #> [1] 0.01 #>  #> $clstgap #> [1] 1e+05 #>  #> $leng #> [1] 15 #>  #> $subSegmSize #> [1] 100 #>  #> $split #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $checkLargest #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $MAFcut #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $CLQmode #> [1] "Density" #>  #> $kin_method #> [1] "chol" #>  #> $digits #> [1] -1 #>  #> $appendrare #> [1] FALSE result$score_table[, c("CLQcut","n_unassigned","n_forced","n_blocks")] #>   CLQcut n_unassigned n_forced n_blocks #> 1   0.45            0        0        9 #> 2   0.55            0        0        9 #> 3   0.65            0        0        9

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
code: library(LDxBlocks)  # Step 1. Open streaming backend (VCF auto-converts to GDS cache on first call) be     <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz")  # Step 2. Prepare phenotype data (BLUEs from mixed model — see Step 2 of this vignette) blues <- read.csv("blues.csv")  # columns: id (or any name), trait1, trait2 ...  # Step 3. Block detection # Use Leiden for WGS panels: polynomial O(n log n) and guaranteed # connected communities (Traag et al. 2019). Louvain can produce # disconnected communities and is not recommended. blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be,   method          = "r2",   CLQcut          = 0.70,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",     # polynomial; guaranteed connected communities   max_bp_distance = 500000L,      # skip pairs > 500 kb; near-O(p)   subSegmSize     = 500L,   leng            = 50L,   n_threads       = 8L ) summarise_blocks(blocks)  # 3. Haplotypes — pass backend directly: streams one chromosome at a time, #    full genome is NEVER loaded into RAM simultaneously. haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps) write_haplotype_diversity(div, "diversity.csv")  feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps,                                         encoding = "additive_012",                                         scale_features = TRUE)  # Numeric dosage matrix: rows=haplotypes, cols=individuals # values: 0/1/2/NA for phased data; 0/1/NA for unphased data write_haplotype_numeric(feat, "hap_matrix_numeric.csv",                          haplotypes = haps, snp_info = be$snp_info)  # Nucleotide character matrix: shows actual allele sequence per individual write_haplotype_character(haplotypes = haps, snp_info = be$snp_info,                            out_file = "hap_matrix_character.txt")  # Decode haplotype strings to nucleotide sequences (for biological interpretation) decoded <- decode_haplotype_strings(haps, snp_info = be$snp_info) head(decoded[, c("block_id","hap_rank","dosage_string",                   "nucleotide_sequence","frequency")])  close_backend(be)

what: 1. Why scale matters: the original Big-LD limitation
title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: original Big_LD() function (Kim et al. 2018) accepts plain R numeric matrix – entire genotype dataset must RAM simultaneously. 500-individual panel 50,000 SNPs works fine. modern breeding programmes operating whole-genome sequencing data, numbers become untenable: 120 GB rice example motivating use case LDxBlocks: second_variant_calling_merged.snpsOnly.maf05.miss20.vcf.gz, 3M-SNP, 204-sample WGS panel original Big-LD require loading entire genotype matrix single block boundary computed.

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: Three features address exponential-blowup /O bottlenecks observed running default parameters 3M+ SNP panels.

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: default CLQmode = "Density" uses Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration, exponential worst-case complexity. 3M-SNP WGS panel CLQcut = 0.5 subSegmSize = 1500, single window can find 4+ million maximal cliques run hours. CLQmode = "Louvain" CLQmode = "Leiden" replace O(n log n) community detection: Edge weights community graph inversely proportional base-pair distance, local LD structure respected. Communities single member become singletons (NA), exactly matching Density path output format.
code: # Recommended for any WGS panel > 500 k SNPs per chromosome blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",    # polynomial — guaranteed connected communities   CLQcut          = 0.70,        # sparser graph reduces community count   max_bp_distance = 500000L,     # sparse LD: skip pairs > 500 kb   subSegmSize     = 500L,        # smaller windows for safety   leng            = 50L,         # narrower boundary scan at WGS density   checkLargest    = TRUE,        # extra guard if Density mode is used   n_threads       = 16L,   min_snps_chr    = 100L )

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: max_bp_distance > 0, SNP pairs within physical distance r² computed via compute_r2_sparse_cpp(). Pairs beyond threshold set 0 adjacency matrix — valid assumption long-range r² decays near-zero WGS density. 500 kb, 70–90% pairs skipped, reducing O(p²) LD computation near-O(p):
code: # 500 kb cutoff: appropriate for most plant and animal WGS panels blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",    # Leiden preferred: guaranteed connected communities   max_bp_distance = 500000L,   CLQcut          = 0.70,   n_threads       = 16L )

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: cases even filtered matrix exceeds available RAM, read_geno_bigmemory() writes genotype matrix binary file (filebacked.big.matrix) wraps standard backend interface. read_chunk() retrieves columns via OS page faults — requested bytes loaded. Storage type "char" (1 byte per cell) saves 8× double 0/1/2 dosage data. Backing files persist across sessions:
code: # Requires: install.packages('bigmemory') be_gds <- read_geno('mydata.gds')  # First run: convert to memory-mapped store (~5 min for 3M SNPs) be_bm <- read_geno_bigmemory(   be_gds,   backingfile = 'mydata_bm',       # .bin + .desc created here   backingpath = '/data/ldxblocks', # persistent directory   type        = 'char',            # 1 byte/cell; 8x smaller than double   verbose     = TRUE ) close_backend(be_gds)  # Subsequent runs: reattach without reloading be_bm <- read_geno_bigmemory(   '/data/ldxblocks/mydata_bm.desc',   snp_info = be_bm$snp_info  # supply separately on reattach )  # All standard functions work identically blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be_bm, CLQmode = 'Leiden', CLQcut = 0.70) close_backend(be_bm)

text: large panels, extract haplotypes one chromosome time avoid loading full genome matrix: chromosome-specific analyses (e.g. single chromosome ):
code: be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.gds") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70,                               n_threads = 16L, verbose = FALSE)  # Pass the backend directly — extract_haplotypes() detects LDxBlocks_backend # and streams one chromosome at a time internally. # Each chromosome is loaded, processed, rm()'d and gc(FALSE) called before # the next chromosome. The full genome is NEVER in RAM simultaneously. haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  # Feature matrix and output (haps is a list of strings, always small in RAM) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps,                                         encoding = "additive_012",                                         scale_features = TRUE) write_haplotype_numeric(feat, "haplotype_matrix_dosage.csv",                          haplotypes = haps, snp_info = be$snp_info) write_haplotype_character(haplotypes = haps, snp_info = be$snp_info,                            out_file = "haplotype_matrix_nucleotide.txt") write_haplotype_diversity(div, "haplotype_diversity.csv")  # Genomic prediction — supply BLUEs in any of the four supported formats. # For format details and ID matching rules, see the Introduction vignette # (Step 2: Phenotype input format) or ?run_haplotype_prediction. blues <- read.csv("blues.csv")  # columns: id, YLD (any column names) pred  <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = as.matrix(read_chunk(be, seq_len(be$n_snps))),   snp_info    = be$snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   blues       = blues,   id_col      = "id",      # name of the ID column in blues   blue_col    = "YLD"      # name of the BLUE column (single trait) )  close_backend(be) # Filter blocks and extract by chromosome — backend still streams haps_chr1 <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks,                                  chr = "1", min_snps = 5)

what: 1. The kinship problem in LD estimation
title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: standard squared Pearson correlation two SNP dosage vectors gjg_j gkg_k : rjk2=[Cov(gj,gk)Var(gj)Var(gk)]2r^2_{jk} = \left[\frac{\mathrm{Cov}(g_j, g_k)}   {\sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(g_j)\,\mathrm{Var}(g_k)}}\right]^2 estimator assumes nn individuals exchangeable — drawn independently population. structured related populations assumption fails. Two individuals share recent common ancestor carry correlated alleles loci, just loci true LD. sample covariance therefore picks genuine recombination-based LD kinship-induced allele sharing, inflating r2r^2 relative true population value. consequence block detection blocks drawn broadly: regions genuinely independent appear correlated sample, clique graph edges , singletons absorbed large blocks.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: Mangin et al. (2012) proposed replacing r2r^2 kinship-adjusted squared correlation rV2rV^2 specifically correct LD estimates structured related populations: rVjk2=[Cor(V−1/2gj,V−1/2gk)]2rV^2_{jk} = \left[\mathrm{Cor}(V^{-1/2} g_j,\; V^{-1/2} g_k)\right]^2 V=ZZ⊤/(2∑jpjqj)V = ZZ^\top / (2\sum_j p_j q_j) VanRaden (2008) GRM (Zij=Gij−2pjZ_{ij} = G_{ij} - 2p_j, GG = raw dosage matrix, pjp_j = allele frequency SNP jj), called KK Mangin et al. (2012) GG VanRaden (2008) . LDxBlocks symbol VV used avoid collision GG raw genotype matrix. V−1/2V^{-1/2} inverse square root. Left-multiplying mean-centred genotype matrix G̃\tilde{G} V−1/2V^{-1/2} removes kinship-induced correlation individuals, resulting correlation measures recombination-based LD.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: kinship matrix VV additive genomic relationship matrix VanRaden (2008), computed AGHmatrix::Gmatrix() inside prepare_geno(): V=ZZ⊤2∑jpjqjV = \frac{ZZ^\top}{2\sum_j p_j q_j} ZZ n×mn \times m frequency-centred genotype matrix elements zij=gij−2pjz_{ij} = g_{ij} - 2p_j (pjp_j = frequency allele A2A_2 SNP jj, qj=1−pjq_j = 1 - p_j). coefficients SNP jj : 0−2pj0 - 2p_j (homozygous A1A1A_1A_1), 1−2pj1 - 2p_j (heterozygous A1A2A_1A_2), 2−2pj2 - 2p_j (homozygous A2A2A_2A_2). scalar denominator 2∑jpjqj2\sum_j p_j q_j scales VV diagonal elements average approximately 1 (resembles numerator relationship matrix outbred population).

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: get_V_inv_sqrt() computes =V−1/2A = V^{-1/2} AVA⊤=IA V ^\top = . Cholesky (kin_method = "chol", default): =R−1A = R^{-1} V=R⊤RV = R^\top R. Fast numerically stable. Eigendecomposition (kin_method = "eigen"): =QΛ−1/2Q⊤= Q \Lambda^{-1/2} Q^\top. Eigenvalues floored 10−610^{-6} stability. Symmetric result; preferable VV near-singular.
code: set.seed(1) G_small <- ldx_geno[, 1:30] Gc      <- scale(G_small, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) V_demo  <- tcrossprod(Gc) / 30 + diag(0.05, nrow(Gc))  # toy PD matrix  A_chol <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_demo, method = "chol") A_eig  <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_demo, method = "eigen")  # Verify: A V A' ≈ I max(abs(A_chol %*% V_demo %*% t(A_chol) - diag(120))) #> [1] 131.4961 max(abs(A_eig  %*% V_demo %*% t(A_eig)  - diag(120))) #> [1] 1.064704e-13

what: 5. Comparing r² and rV² on example data
title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: r² (left) vs rV² (right) first 25 SNPs chr1
code: idx_25 <- 1:25 r2_mat <- compute_r2(ldx_geno[, idx_25])  # rV² with a toy kinship (normally produced by prepare_geno) Gc_25  <- scale(ldx_geno[, idx_25], center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) V_toy  <- tcrossprod(Gc_25) / 25 + diag(0.1, 120) A_toy  <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_toy) X_whit <- A_toy %*% Gc_25 rv2_mat <- compute_rV2(X_whit)  cat("Mean r² :", round(mean(r2_mat[upper.tri(r2_mat)]),  4), "\n") #> Mean r² : 0.8892 cat("Mean rV²:", round(mean(rv2_mat[upper.tri(rv2_mat)]), 4), "\n") #> Mean rV²: 0.9398 par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1)) image(r2_mat,   main = "Standard r²",       col = hcl.colors(20, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE),       xlab = "SNP", ylab = "SNP") image(rv2_mat,  main = "Kinship-adjusted rV²",       col = hcl.colors(20, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE),       xlab = "SNP", ylab = "SNP") par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Linkage disequilibrium (LD) block detection foundational step modern genomic analyses. Knowing SNPs co-segregate unit determines GWAS results interpreted, haplotypes defined population genetics, genomic prediction models structured. Despite importance, implementations LD block detection suffer three problems limit usefulness practice. kinship problem. Classical LD estimators (r²) assume independence individuals. livestock, crop, family-based human cohorts assumption systematically violated. Cryptic relatedness inflates pairwise correlations, causing LD appear stronger blocks drawn broadly wrong places. kinship-adjusted squared correlation rV² (Mangin et al. 2012, Heredity 108:285-291) corrects whitening genotype matrix inverse square root genomic relationship matrix (GRM): rVij2=Cov(Xiv,Xjv)2Var(Xiv)Var(Xjv)rV^2_{ij} = \frac{\mathrm{Cov}(X^v_i,\, X^v_j)^2}{\mathrm{Var}(X^v_i)\,\mathrm{Var}(X^v_j)} Xv=V−1/2G̃X^v = V^{-1/2}\tilde{G} kinship-whitened, mean-centred genotype matrix; V=ZZ⊤/(2∑jpjqj)V = ZZ^\top / (2\sum_j p_j q_j) VanRaden (2008) GRM (Zij=Gij−2pjZ_{ij} = G_{ij} - 2p_j = frequency-centred raw dosage, qj=1−pjq_j = 1-p_j); V−1/2V^{-1/2} inverse square root (Mangin et al. 2012, eq. 1–2). Subscripts ii jj index SNPs. equivalent [Cor(Xiv,Xjv)]2[\mathrm{Cor}(X^v_i, X^v_j)]^2. Notation. GG denotes raw n×mn\times m dosage matrix (Gij∈{0,1,2}G_{ij} \\{0,1,2\}); G̃\tilde{G} mean-centred form (G̃ij=Gij−G‾j\tilde{G}_{ij} = G_{ij}-\bar{G}_j, used LD computation); ZZ frequency-centred form (Zij=Gij−2pjZ_{ij} = G_{ij}-2p_j, used GRM). VV denotes GRM avoid collision GG dosage matrix. Mangin (2012) paper GRM called KK equivalent VV ; VanRaden (2008) calls GG. Every pairwise correlation Big-LD algorithm replaced rV², giving block boundaries reflect true recombination structure rather population-structure artefacts. scale problem. original Big-LD implementation (Kim et al. 2018) contains compiled code – every matrix operation runs R interpreter. modern whole-genome sequencing panels 2-10 million markers inner loops prohibitively slow, loading full genotype matrix detection impossible workstations. LDxBlocks addresses C++/Armadillo computational core compiled via Rcpp, OpenMP-parallelised LD computation, unified multi-format /O layer, strict never-full-genome memory model: full genotype matrix never loaded RAM simultaneously regardless dataset size format. pipeline gap. original Big-LD stops block boundaries. breeding programmes GWAS follow-studies immediate next questions : haplotypes exist within block? diverse ? blocks harbour QTLs? build genomic prediction model captures multi-locus block effects? questions require coherent downstream pipeline original implementation provide. LDxBlocks tools? Unlike PLINK --blocks, LDxBlocks uses clique-based Big-LD segmentation algorithm require pre-specified number blocks naturally handles complex LD patterns. also supports kinship correction (rV²) six input formats natively. Unlike original Big-LD R package, LDxBlocks compiled C++ core (approximately 40x faster typical window sizes), streams genotype data disk without loading full genome RAM, adds rV² kinship correction, handles singleton SNPs explicitly, extends pipeline haplotype analysis genomic prediction. Unlike LDstore2 LDpred2, LDxBlocks produces interpretable genomic intervals (start/end position, rsID) output — just summary statistics — making blocks immediately usable annotation, diversity analysis, region-based modelling. Unlike gpart (Bioconductor successor Big-LD), LDxBlocks integrates directly R-based genomic prediction workflows (rrBLUP, BGLR, ASReml-R) haplotype feature matrix module, supports automatic GDS conversion WGS-scale datasets, provides GWAS-driven parameter auto-tuning.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks complete pipeline genome-wide LD block detection related structured populations, downstream haplotype analysis genomic prediction utilities. extends Big-LD clique-based segmentation algorithm Kim et al. (2018) 15 concrete improvements: Dual LD metric — standard r² (default, fast, kinship correction, suitable 10 M+ markers) kinship-adjusted rV² (structured related populations) selectable via single method = argument. C++/Armadillo core — seven compiled functions handle r² computation, adjacency matrix construction, MAF filtering, boundary scanning, sparse LD, column-wise correlation. full r² matrix 1,500-SNP window computes milliseconds rather seconds. OpenMP parallelism — outer loop compute_r2_cpp() parallelised across threads; count controlled n_threads =. Unified multi-format /O — read_geno() auto-detects reads numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF/VCF.gz, SNPRelate GDS, PLINK BED/BIM/FAM, -memory R matrices single entry point common backend interface (LDxBlocks_backend). Never-full-genome memory model — full genotype matrix never held RAM format. Numeric dosage CSV read pre-allocated 50,000-row chunks (peak RAM = one chunk, 2× file). VCF HapMap auto-convert streaming GDS cache. GDS PLINK BED backends load SNP window per CLQD call. gc(FALSE) called chromosome prevent heap fragmentation across 20–30 chromosome passes. MAF filter C++ — maf_filter_cpp() runs single O(np) pass, handling NA imputation monomorphic detection simultaneously. C++ boundary scan — boundary_scan_cpp() replaces R inner loop subsegmentation step runs hundreds times per chromosome, eliminating interpreter overhead -called function pipeline. Sparse r² computation — compute_r2_sparse_cpp() computes pairwise r² SNP pairs within user-specified bp distance, avoiding O(p²) cost large sub-segments distant SNPs always threshold. Automatic parameter tuning — tune_LD_params() performs grid search CLQcut parameters, selecting combination minimises unassigned forced GWAS marker placements. Haplotype reconstruction — extract_haplotypes() constructs phase-free diploid allele strings per LD block individual, nearly 1:1 true haplotype classes within high-LD blocks. Diversity metrics — compute_haplotype_diversity() computes per-block richness, Nei’s expected heterozygosity, Shannon entropy, dominant haplotype frequency. Prediction feature matrix — build_haplotype_feature_matrix() converts haplotype strings numeric dosage columns GBLUP, BayesB, machine learning models, capturing multi-locus epistatic effects implicitly. Polynomial community detection — CLQmode = "Louvain" CLQmode = "Leiden" replace Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration O(n log n) community detection, eliminating exponential blowup dense WGS panels. network community detection algorithms applied SNP LD graph place exact clique enumeration; citations follow original algorithm publications (Blondel et al. 2008; Traag et al. 2019). Sparse LD computation — max_bp_distance parameter restricts r² SNP pairs within physical distance window via compute_r2_sparse_cpp(), reducing O(p²) near-O(p) WGS panels (70–90% pairs skipped 500 kb). Memory-mapped genotype store — read_geno_bigmemory() wraps source bigmemory::big.matrix backed binary file; read_chunk() loads OS pages requested columns, keeping peak RAM proportional n_samples × subSegmSize × 8 bytes.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Algorithm. Enumerates maximal cliques LD graph via Bron-Kerbosch algorithm (igraph::max_cliques()). clique scored : score(𝒦)=|𝒦|spankb(𝒦)+1\text{score}(\mathcal{K}) = \frac{|\mathcal{K}|}{\mathrm{span_{kb}}(\mathcal{K}) + 1} |𝒦||\mathcal{K}| number SNPs spankb\mathrm{span_{kb}} physical span kilobases. greedy assignment iteratively selects highest-scoring clique, removes SNPs, repeats SNPs assigned cliques remain. Biological interpretation. Divides physical span, compact 10-SNP block spanning 5 kb scores higher diffuse 10-SNP block spanning 50 kb. favours tight, physically co-located LD blocks correspond genuine recombination-suppressed intervals. original scoring used Kim et al. (2018) appropriate whenever clique enumeration computationally feasible. Pros. - Biologically motivated: rewards compact, high-density LD structure. - Exact — every maximal clique found scored. - Well-studied validated original Big-LD benchmark. - Blocks reflect true recombination structure rather graph topology. Cons. - Bron-Kerbosch exponential worst-case complexity. dense WGS LD graphs single 1,500-SNP window can enumerate millions cliques run hours fail complete. - Completely impractical WGS panels without max_bp_distance pruning. use. Array-chip panels (< 100k SNPs per chromosome), panel subSegmSize windows fewer ~200 SNPs average, biological interpretability scoring function matters compute time constraint.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection

Biological interpretation. Maximises number co-inherited SNPs per block, without penalising physically dispersed cliques. 20-SNP block spanning 200 kb scores higher 15-SNP block spanning 5 kb. appropriate goal capture many co-inherited markers possible per block, example sparse chip panels blocks large. Pros. - Maximises haplotype block membership — block captures SNPs. - Useful sparse panels compact-density scoring fragment blocks. Cons. - exponential worst-case complexity Density — equally impractical WGS panels. - Can produce physically large, diffuse blocks span multiple genuine recombination intervals, reducing biological resolution. use. title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Algorithm. Replaces Bron-Kerbosch modularity-maximising community detection (igraph::cluster_louvain(); Blondel et al. 2008). Edges weighted physical proximity (weight = 1 / (bp_distance_kb + 1)) nearby SNPs preferentially grouped. Runs O(n log n). Known weakness. Traag et al. (2019) proved Louvain can produce internally disconnected communities: two subsets SNPs can assigned community despite LD path connecting within community. LD block detection correctness problem — block must contiguous genomic interval. LDxBlocks applies mandatory connectivity post-processing step Louvain: community tested internal connectivity via igraph::is_connected(); disconnected communities split connected components receives new community ID. fix prevents malformed blocks adds O(communities) overhead means LDxBlocks Louvain output pure Louvain — Louvain connectivity repair step. Pros. - Substantially faster Bron-Kerbosch dense WGS windows. - Practical WGS panels used max_bp_distance. Cons. - guarantee connected communities without post-processing fix. - connectivity repair adds overhead means output may differ reference Louvain implementation. - Leiden achieves polynomial speed formal guarantee, making Louvain strictly dominated Leiden application. use. speed absolute priority, Leiden unavailable (older igraph versions), benchmarking connectivity-fixed Louvain output. cases, prefer Leiden.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Algorithm. Replaces Bron-Kerbosch Leiden algorithm (igraph::cluster_leiden(); Traag et al. 2019). Leiden improves Louvain adding refinement phase guarantees every detected community internally well-connected. physical-proximity edge weighting applied Louvain. Runs O(n log n). Key guarantee. Every community produced Leiden connected subgraph LD graph. LD block detection means every block guaranteed correspond contiguous, connected set SNPs LD paths members. post-processing connectivity fix needed. Pros. - Polynomial O(n log n) — never exhibits exponential blowup dense graphs. - Formally guaranteed connected communities (Traag et al. 2019). - post-processing required — output correct construction. - igraph interface comparable speed Louvain. - Strictly superior Louvain LD block detection: complexity, stronger guarantees. Cons. - Requires igraph >= 1.3.0 (available since 2021; standard platforms). - Community boundaries determined modularity optimisation, original Big-LD density scoring function — blocks may occasionally differ Density/Maximal output chip-density panels. - enumerate cliques explicitly, clstgap-based gap splitting applied post-step (split = TRUE) rather integral scoring. use. WGS panels (2M+ SNPs), panel Bron-Kerbosch takes > 1 minute, default whenever unsure. combination CLQmode = "Leiden" + max_bp_distance = 500000L recommended configuration 3M-SNP WGS panels.

code: blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   # ── Genotype input ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   geno_matrix  = be,                  # LDxBlocks_backend or plain matrix    # ── LD metric ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   method       = "r2",                # "r2" (default) or "rV2"   kin_method   = "chol",              # "chol" (default) or "eigen" for rV2    # ── Clique detection ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   CLQcut       = 0.70,                # r2 threshold for graph edges   CLQmode      = "Density",           # "Density"/"Maximal" (exact, Bron-Kerbosch)                                       # or "Louvain"/"Leiden" (polynomial, WGS-safe)   clstgap      = 40000L,              # max bp gap within clique (split=TRUE)   split        = FALSE,               # split cliques at genomic gaps    # ── Subsegmentation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   leng         = 200L,                # boundary-scan half-window (SNPs)   subSegmSize  = 1500L,               # max SNPs per CLQD call    # ── Filtering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   MAFcut       = 0.05,                # minor allele frequency minimum   appendrare   = FALSE,               # append rare SNPs after detection    # ── Large window heuristics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────   checkLargest = FALSE,               # dense-core pre-pass for >= 500 SNPs    # ── Parallelism and precision ────────────────────────────────────────────────   n_threads    = 8L,                  # OpenMP threads for C++ LD kernel   digits       = -1L,                 # -1 = no rounding (default)    # ── Chromosome minimum ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────   min_snps_chr = 10L,                 # skip chromosomes with fewer SNPs    # ── Reproducibility ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   seed         = 42L,   verbose      = TRUE )

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks enforces strict memory contract: full genotype matrix never held RAM dataset size format. Numeric dosage CSV — Two-pass chunked reading following OptSLDP pattern (Akohoue et al. 2026): Pass 1 scans header counts rows zero data loading. single pre-allocated matrix filled 50,000-row chunks via successive data.table::fread() calls. Peak RAM = one chunk (2× file). gc(FALSE) called chunk. VCF HapMap — Auto-converted SNPRelate GDS cache first call (placed next source file; .gds extension). Subsequent calls reuse cache automatically. access streaming via read_chunk(). GDS PLINK BED — read_chunk(backend, col_idx) called per sub-segment per chromosome. subSegmSize = 1500 50,000 SNPs per chromosome, approximately 33 disk reads per chromosome. read loads 1,500-column slice; rest genome remains disk. Chromosome loop — run_Big_LD_all_chr() extract_haplotypes(), chromosome extracted, processed, freed rm(), gc(FALSE) called next chromosome touched, preventing heap fragmentation accumulating across 20–30 chromosome passes.

title: Backsolve SNP Effects from GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) — backsolve_snp_effects
text: Derives per-SNP additive effect estimates genome-wide genomic estimated breeding values (GEBV) without re-fitting marker model. implements Step 2 Tong et al. (2025) haplotype stacking pipeline: $$\hat{\alpha} = \frac{M^\top G^{-1} \hat{g}}{2 \sum_t p_t(1-p_t)}$$ \(M\) centred genotype matrix, \(G\) VanRaden GRM, \(\hat{g}\) GEBV, \(p_t\) allele frequencies.

what: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
text: Core per-chromosome LD block detection. Two LD metrics supported: method = "r2" (default) Standard squared Pearson     correlation, computed C++ Armadillo kernel. kinship matrix     required. Suitable large datasets (hundreds thousands     millions markers) unstructured mildly structured populations. method = "rV2" Kinship-adjusted squared correlation     (Kim et al. 2018). Requires computing inverting GRM via     AGHmatrix + ASRgenomics. Recommended highly related populations     (livestock, inbred lines) moderate marker counts (< 200 k per chr). genome-wide analyses use run_Big_LD_all_chr. automatic parameter tuning use tune_LD_params.

title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) m <- 80; b1 <- 60; b2 <- 60 make_block <- function(m, size, p, flip = 0.03) {   seed_snp <- rbinom(m, 2, p)   M <- matrix(seed_snp, nrow = m, ncol = size)   for (j in seq_len(size)) {     idx <- sample.int(m, max(1L, floor(flip * m)))     M[idx, j] <- pmin(2L, pmax(0L, M[idx, j] + sample(c(-1L, 1L), length(idx), TRUE)))   }   M } G <- cbind(make_block(m, b1, 0.30), make_block(m, b2, 0.60)) colnames(G) <- paste0("rs", seq_len(ncol(G))) rownames(G) <- paste0("ind", seq_len(nrow(G))) pos <- c(seq(1, by = 1000, length.out = b1), seq(5e6, by = 1000, length.out = b2)) SNPinfo <- data.frame(SNP = colnames(G), POS = pos) blocks <- LDxBlocks:::Big_LD(G, SNPinfo, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.6,                  leng = 30, subSegmSize = 120, verbose = FALSE) head(blocks) } # }

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
text: Partitions SNPs genomic window LD cliques. Three algorithms available via CLQmode: "Density" (default) Standard Big-LD clique scoring – edges     built full r? matrix, cliques found Bron-Kerbosch     (igraph::max_cliques), scored density = size / span.     Exact, exponential worst-case. Use checkLargest = TRUE     guard blowup dense WGS panels. "Maximal" algorithm Density scored clique     size (original Big-LD maximality criterion). "Louvain" Louvain community detection     (igraph::cluster_louvain). Polynomial time O(n log n) – never     exponential. Recommended WGS panels (>500 SNPs per window)     time CLQmode = "Density" slow. Communities returned     bin IDs matching Density output format downstream code     unaffected. "Leiden" Leiden community detection     (igraph::cluster_leiden). Stricter Louvain – produces     well-connected communities without disconnected nodes. Requires     igraph >= 1.3.0. Slightly slower Louvain higher quality     communities. Recommended Louvain resolution matters. max_bp_distance supplied (> 0), SNP pairs within physical distance r? computed (compute_r2_sparse_cpp). reduces O(p?) LD computation near-O(p) WGS panels long-range LD negligible (typically > 500 kb). Applied clique algorithm.

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
text: subgeno Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), 0/1/2 coded. subSNPinfo Data frame: col 1 = rsID, col 2 = bp position. adj_subgeno n x p matrix prepare_geno(). CLQcut Numeric (0,1]. LD threshold edges. Default 0.5. clstgap Integer. Max bp gap within clique (split=TRUE). CLQmode Algorithm: "Density" (default, exact, exponential worst-case), "Maximal" (exact, maximality criterion), "Louvain" (polynomial, recommended WGS), "Leiden" (polynomial, stricter communities, igraph >= 1.3.0). codechange Logical. Sign-flip heuristic. Default FALSE. checkLargest Logical. Dense-core pre-pass >= 500 SNPs using Density Maximal mode. Default FALSE. effect CLQmode Louvain Leiden (already polynomial). split Logical. Split cliques gaps. Default FALSE. digits Integer. Rounding LD. -1 = none (default). n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads. Default 1. max_bp_distance Integer. Maximum base-pair distance SNP pair r? computed. Pairs beyond distance assumed negligible LD set 0. Default 0L = disabled (compute pairs, original behaviour). Recommended value WGS panels: 500000L (500 kb). effect window spans less max_bp_distance. verbose Logical.

what: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
text: LD block, computes local GEBV (haplotype effect) every individual summing per-SNP additive effects alleles carry within block: $$\text{local GEBV}_f = \sum_{t \f} \left[ h_t \alpha_{1t} +   (1-h_t)\alpha_{0t} \right]$$ \(h_t \\{0, 0.5, 1\}\) allele dosage SNP \(t\) scaled [0,1], \(\alpha_{1t}\) ALT allele effect, \(\alpha_{0t} = -\alpha_{1t}\) REF allele effect (symmetry additive model). Blocks ranked Var(local GEBV) – blocks high variance contribute strongly trait differences among individuals likely harbour causal loci (Tong et al. 2025).

what: When to use r^2 vs rV^2
title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
text: r^2 Use large unstructured datasets (> 500 k markers), random     mating populations, whenever speed matters. standard estimator     inflated related populations (.e. -estimate LD)     usually leads slightly conservative (larger) blocks rather     catastrophically wrong ones. rV^2 Use highly structured / related populations (livestock,     inbred lines, family-based human cohorts) kinship inflation     meaningfully distort block boundaries. Requires computing     inverting GRM - prohibitive beyond ~5 k individuals.

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: multiple SNPs within block GWAS-significant, marginal BETA values correlated (due LD) summed directly. LDxBlocks returns three complementary columns BETA present gwas_results: lead_beta BETA lead (lowest-p) SNP. simplest     proxy block effect – assumes lead SNP fully tags     causal variant. Underestimates block effect multiple     independent signals exist. sig_snps Semicolon-separated IDs significant SNPs     block. Pass conditional/joint analysis tool (e.g.     COJO, SuSiE, finemap) obtain independent within-block effects. sig_betas Semicolon-separated marginal BETA values     significant SNPs (order sig_snps). absolute     values upper bound true block effect     include LD-induced inflation; use lead_beta joint analysis     calibrated estimates. biologically correct block-level effect, fit haplotype model: build_haplotype_feature_matrix() produces 0/1/2 dosage columns whose regression coefficients (alpha_h) true per-allele effects.

title: Integrate GWAS QTL Regions with Haplotype Prediction Results — integrate_gwas_haplotypes
text: Three complementary evidence layers combined per block: Biological (GWAS) block contain genome-wide significant     marker? Sourced define_qtl_regions(). Statistical (variance) block explain substantial variance     trait? Blocks scaled Var(local GEBV) >= 0.9     flagged important run_haplotype_prediction(). Diversity () block enough haplotype diversity     stack favourable alleles? Sourced     compute_haplotype_diversity(). output priority_score sum binary flags layer (0-3). Blocks scoring 3 supported three lines evidence strongest candidates haplotype stacking. Blocks scoring 2 worth investigating. Blocks scoring 1 0 require caution.

what: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: Fast, scalable linkage disequilibrium (LD) block detection genome-wide SNP data using C++/Armadillo computational core two LD metrics: standard r^2 kinship-adjusted rV^2. Includes haplotype reconstruction, diversity metrics, prediction feature matrices, multi-format genotype /O.

title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: r^2 (method = "r2") Standard squared Pearson correlation.     kinship matrix required. C++ kernel via RcppArmadillo + OpenMP.     Suitable unstructured populations large datasets (10 M+ markers). rV^2 (method = "rV2") Kinship-whitened squared correlation     (Mangin et al. 2012, Heredity 108:285-291). Corrects LD inflation relatedness. livestock,     inbred lines, family-based cohorts < 200 k markers per chromosome.

title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: Read genotype data: read_geno – auto-detects CSV,     HapMap, VCF, GDS, BED, plain matrix.     HapMap, VCF, GDS, BED, plain matrix. WGS panels peak RAM     concern, use read_geno_bigmemory build     memory-mapped store (requires bigmemory). Detect LD blocks chromosome-wise: run_Big_LD_all_chr. Optionally auto-tune parameters: tune_LD_params. Reconstruct haplotypes: extract_haplotypes. Decode haplotype strings nucleotides:     decode_haplotype_strings. Compute diversity metrics: compute_haplotype_diversity. Build prediction feature matrix:     build_haplotype_feature_matrix. Map GWAS hits QTL regions: define_qtl_regions. Genomic prediction (Tong et al. 2024/2025):     run_haplotype_prediction – full pipeline BLUEs     block importance; step--step via     compute_haplotype_grm,     backsolve_snp_effects,     compute_local_gebv,     rank_haplotype_blocks. Write outputs: write_haplotype_numeric,     write_haplotype_character,     write_haplotype_diversity. run everything : run_ldx_pipeline.

title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
text: 230 SNPs divided across three chromosomes (80, 80, 70 SNPs respectively). chromosome contains three distinct LD blocks separated short stretches (5 SNPs) low-LD singletons 50 kb inter-block gap: Chromosome 1 Three blocks 25, 20, 25 SNPs. Chromosome 2 Three blocks 30, 20, 20 SNPs. Chromosome 3 Three blocks 20, 20, 20 SNPs. Within block, SNPs generated copying common founder haplotype applying random single-allele flips (flip rate 4%) SNP independently, producing strong within-block LD (r^2 > 0.7 pairs). -block SNPs simulated independently binomial distributions randomised allele frequencies produce near-zero LD.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: path Character. Path genotype file, R numeric matrix format = "matrix". format Character NULL. One "numeric", "hapmap", "vcf", "gds", "bed", "matrix". NULL (default) auto-detects extension. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS (optionally REF, ALT). Required format = "matrix". sample_ids Character vector. Override sample IDs extracted file. Length must equal number samples. sep Character. Field separator "numeric" format. na_strings Character vector. Strings treated NA. Default c("NA", "N", "NN", "./.", ".", ""). gds_cache Character NULL. Path GDS cache file written format = "vcf" SNPRelate available. NULL (default), GDS file placed next VCF .gds extension. Set FALSE disable auto-conversion read VCF fully memory instead. cache file already exists reused without re-converting (fast subsequent calls). Ignored non-VCF formats. Default ",". clean_malformed Logical. TRUE, input file stream-cleaned reading: line whose delimiter-separated column count match header silently removed. adds one extra streaming pass file required files produced variant callers (e.g. NGSEP) embed extra characters FORMAT fields. Applies numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF formats. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: "numeric" CSV TXT, one row per SNP. Required columns:     SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT. Remaining     columns sample dosages {0, 1, 2, NA}. Extension: .csv,     .txt. "hapmap" Standard 11-column HapMap header followed sample     columns two-character nucleotide calls (AA, ,     NN missing). Extension: .hmp.txt. "vcf" VCF v4.2. phased (0|1) unphased     (0/1) GT fields accepted. Multi-allelic sites use first ALT.     Missing (./.) becomes NA. Extension: .vcf,     .vcf.gz. "gds" SNPRelate GDS file. Requires     BiocManager::install("SNPRelate"). Extension: .gds. "bed" PLINK binary BED. Companion .bim     .fam files must exist path stem. Extension:     .bed. "matrix" -memory R numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs,     0/1/2). Must supply snp_info separately.

text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs; values 0/1/2), spanning chromosomes. snp_info Data frame columns CHR, SNP, POS. Column order flexible; columns matched name. CLQcut, clstgap, leng, subSegmSize, MAFcut, appendrare, checkLargest, CLQmode, kin_method, split, digits, seed, verbose Forwarded Big_LD. See function's documentation details. method Character. LD metric: "r2" (default, standard squared Pearson correlation) "rV2" (kinship-adjusted). See Big_LD details. n_threads Integer. Number OpenMP threads C++ LD kernel. Default 1L. Increase multi-core systems. singleton_as_block Logical. TRUE, SNPs pass MAF filtering assigned clique returned single-SNP blocks (start == end, length_bp == 1). Default FALSE. See Big_LD details. min_snps_chr Integer. Chromosomes fewer SNPs MAF filtering skipped. Default 10L. Increase skip small scaffolds whole-genome datasets. chr Character vector NULL. supplied, named chromosomes processed. Labels must match values snp_info$CHR normalisation (chr prefix). E.g. chr = c("1","3","5") chr = "X". Default NULL processes chromosomes. clean_malformed Logical. TRUE, stream-clean input file reading removing lines whose column count match header. relevant geno_matrix file path wrapped backend. Default FALSE. max_bp_distance Integer. Maximum base-pair distance SNP pair r\(^2\) computed. Pairs beyond distance set zero adjacency matrix (assumed negligible LD). 0L (default) disables computes pairs (original behaviour). Recommended value WGS panels: 500000L (500 kb). effect CLQmode "Louvain" "Leiden" window spans less max_bp_distance. Requires sorted SNP positions within sub-segment (guaranteed run_Big_LD_all_chr).

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: single-call wrapper takes one genotype file produces complete haplotype-based dataset ready genomic prediction. Internally handles format detection, optional GDS conversion large files, MAF filtering, genome-wide LD block detection, haplotype extraction, diversity analysis, output writing. user provides input file path desired output paths. intermediate steps run transparently.

code: run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source,   out_blocks,   out_diversity,   out_hap_matrix,   hap_format = c("numeric", "character"),   maf_cut = 0.05,   CLQcut = 0.5,   method = c("r2", "rV2"),   kin_method = "chol",   CLQmode = c("Density", "Maximal", "Louvain", "Leiden"),   leng = 200L,   subSegmSize = 1500L,   clstgap = 40000L,   split = FALSE,   appendrare = FALSE,   singleton_as_block = FALSE,   checkLargest = FALSE,   digits = -1L,   n_threads = 1L,   min_snps_chr = 10L,   min_snps_block = 3L,   top_n = NULL,   min_freq = 0.01,   scale_hap_matrix = FALSE,   chr = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   max_bp_distance = 0L,   clean_malformed = FALSE,   use_bigmemory = FALSE,   bigmemory_path = tempdir(),   bigmemory_type = "char" )

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: geno_source Path genotype file, LDxBlocks_backend object already created read_geno read_geno_bigmemory. Passing backend skips internal read_geno() call, allowing use pre-built backend including file-backed bigmemory stores. Supported file formats path supplied: numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF/VCF.gz, SNPRelate GDS, PLINK BED. dosage CSV (`.csv`), HapMap (`.hmp.txt`), VCF (`.vcf`, `.vcf.gz`), SNPRelate GDS (`.gds`), PLINK BED (`.bed`). Format detected automatically file extension. out_blocks Path LD block table CSV. Columns: `CHR`, `start`, `end`, `start.rsID`, `end.rsID`, `start.bp`, `end.bp`, `length_bp`, `length_snps`, `block_name`. out_diversity Path haplotype diversity table CSV. Columns: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps, n_ind, n_haplotypes, (Nei 1973 corrected), Shannon, n_eff_alleles (effective number alleles = 1/\(\sum p_i^2\)), freq_dominant, sweep_flag (TRUE freq_dominant >= 0.90, indicating possible selective sweep strong founder effect), phased. out_hap_matrix Path haplotype genotype matrix. Format controlled `hap_format`. See section **Haplotype genotype matrix**. hap_format Output format haplotype matrix: "numeric" (default) - Tab-delimited. Rows = haplotype     alleles, columns = individuals. Metadata columns: hap_id,     CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps,     alleles (nucleotide sequence allele), frequency.     Individual columns contain 0/1/2/NA dosage. "character" - Tab-delimited. row/column orientation.     Individual cells contain nucleotide sequence haplotype     allele individual carries , "-" absent, "."     missing. maf_cut Minimum minor allele frequency. SNPs threshold removed block detection. Default `0.05`. CLQcut r^2 threshold clique edges CLQD. Higher values produce tighter, smaller blocks. Default `0.5`. method LD metric: "r2" (default) "rV2" (requires kinship matrix; see run_Big_LD_all_chr). kin_method Whitening method rV2: "chol" (Cholesky, default, faster) "eigen" (eigendecomposition, stable near-singular kinship matrices). CLQmode Clique scoring mode: "Density" (default, prefers compact high-density cliques – recommended analyses) "Maximal" (prefers largest cliques regardless span). leng Boundary scan half-window SNPs. Default `200L`. Reduce 50-100 dense WGS panels. subSegmSize Maximum SNPs per CLQD sub-segment. Controls peak RAM: `subSegmSize x n_individuals x 8` bytes. Default `1500L`. clstgap Maximum base-pair gap allowed within clique split. Default 40000L (40 kb). Increase populations long-range LD (e.g. inbred lines); decrease high-recombination panels. split Logical. TRUE, split cliques whose SNP span exceeds clstgap bp largest internal gap. Default FALSE. appendrare Logical. TRUE, SNPs fail MAF filtering appended nearest block detection. Default FALSE. singleton_as_block Logical. TRUE, SNPs receive clique assignment (singletons recombination hotspots) returned single-SNP blocks block table. Default FALSE (original Big-LD behaviour – singletons silently dropped). checkLargest Logical. TRUE, apply dense-core pre-pass clique enumeration sub-segments >=500 SNPs prevent exponential blowup. Default FALSE. digits Integer. Round r^2 values many decimal places clique detection. -1L (default) disables rounding. n_threads OpenMP threads C++ LD kernel. Default `1L`. min_snps_chr Skip chromosomes fewer post-filter SNPs . Default `10L`. Increase skip unplaced scaffolds. min_snps_block Minimum SNPs per haplotype block. Blocks smaller excluded haplotype analysis. Default `3L`. top_n Integer NULL. Maximum haplotype alleles per block output matrix. NULL (default) retains alleles min_freq – cap. Set integer (e.g. 5L) limit column count memory reasons. min_freq Minimum haplotype allele frequency (0–1). Alleles observed lower frequency threshold dropped building feature matrix output files. Default 0.01 (1%). Rare alleles threshold estimated reliably typical training sets add noise. Lower values retain rare alleles; higher values (e.g. 0.05) match MAF filter applied SNPs. scale_hap_matrix Logical. `TRUE`, scale haplotype matrix columns zero mean unit variance writing. Useful GBLUP-style models. Default `FALSE`. chr Character vector chromosome names process. `NULL` (default) processes chromosomes. verbose Logical. Print timestamped progress. Default `TRUE`. max_bp_distance Integer. Maximum bp distance SNP pair r\(^2\) computed LD graph. 0L (default) computes pairs. Recommended WGS panels: 500000L (500 kb). Reduces O(p\(^2\)) LD computation near-O(p) set. clean_malformed Logical. Stream-clean input file reading removing lines whose column count match header. Needed files NGSEP variant callers. Default FALSE. use_bigmemory Logical. TRUE geno_source file path, source file first loaded file-backed bigmemory::big.matrix block detection. OS pages needed sub-segment window loaded RAM, keeping peak memory proportional n_samples x subSegmSize rather full genome. Requires bigmemory package. Default FALSE. Ignored geno_source already LDxBlocks_backend (supplied backend used -). bigmemory_path Directory bigmemory backing files (.bin .desc) SNP info cache (_snpinfo.rds) written read . Defaults tempdir() files cleaned end R session. Supply persistent directory (e.g. next output CSVs) want reattach backing file restart without re-reading VCF. bigmemory_type Storage type big.matrix: "char" (1 byte per cell, 8x smaller double, sufficient 0/1/2 dosage), "short" (2 bytes), "double" (8 bytes). Default "char".

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: use_bigmemory = TRUE pipeline converts source file bigmemory::big.matrix backed two binary files disk running analysis. OS pages needed sub-segment window loaded RAM; rest genome stays disk. Peak RAM proportional n_samples x subSegmSize x bytes_per_cell rather full genome matrix. Backing files created bigmemory_path: ldxblocks_bm.bin Raw binary genotype data.     Size = n_samples x n_snps x bytes_per_cell.     type = "char" 204 samples x 3M SNPs: ~0.6 GB. ldxblocks_bm.desc Tiny text descriptor bigmemory     uses memory-map .bin file. Never edit manually. ldxblocks_bm_snpinfo.rds Cached SNP metadata (SNP, CHR,     POS, REF, ALT). bigmemory store metadata, file     saved separately enable restart without re-reading source file. Restart behaviour: three files already exist bigmemory_path pipeline called, .bin reattached instantly via bigmemory::attach.big.matrix() – source VCF touched. force rebuild, delete .bin .desc files. Choosing bigmemory_type: "char" (default, recommended) 1 signed byte per cell     (range –128..127). Genotype dosage values 0, 1, 2 fit without loss.     8x smaller "double". "short" 2 bytes per cell. needed 0/1/2 dosage;     use pipeline stores values outside –128..127     matrix. "double" 8 bytes per cell. standard R     matrix(). Use downstream code requires double     precision RAM saving needed. use use_bigmemory = TRUE: Peak RAM GDS streaming exceeds available node memory. need fast restart: .bin persists across R sessions     (supply persistent bigmemory_path, tempdir()). Multiple pipeline runs share panel (one .bin,     many readers – bigmemory memory-maps safe concurrent access).

VCF, HapMap, numeric CSV files backend reads one chromosome window time - peak RAM equals one `subSegmSize`-SNP window regardless total marker count. large files (> 2 M SNPs) GDS backend via `SNPRelate` used automatically `SNPRelate` package installed.

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: output formats haplotype alleles rows individuals columns, preceded metadata columns: hap_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps, alleles (nucleotide sequence allele), frequency. "numeric": individual cells haplotype dosage values: Phased data: 0/1/2/NA – 0 = neither gamete carries         allele, 1 = one gamete carries (heterozygous),         2 = gametes carry (homozygous). Unphased data: 0/1/NA – 0 = absent, 1 = present         (individual's block-level string matches allele exactly).         value 2 used unphased data two         chromosomes distinguished – individual homozygous         allele one heterozygous produce different         observable strings treated different alleles. Compatible rrBLUP, BGLR, sommer, ASReml-R. "character": individual cells full nucleotide     sequence allele (e.g. "AGTTA") individual     carries , "-" absent, "." missing.     Heterozygous positions use IUPAC ambiguity codes     (R=/G, Y=C/T, S=G/C, W=/T, K=G/T, M=/C).

what: Auto-Tune LD Block Detection Parameters — tune_LD_params
title: Auto-Tune LD Block Detection Parameters — tune_LD_params
text: Performs grid search Big_LD parameters selects combination minimises, order priority: Unassigned GWAS markers (markers falling block). Forced assignments (nearest-block fall-back). Number blocks (parsimony). Deviation target_bp_band (biological plausibility). prefer_perfect = TRUE (default), combinations achieving zero unassigned zero forced assignments prioritised among . selecting best parameter set, tune_LD_params runs run_Big_LD_all_chr chromosomes assigns every GWAS marker block, returning final blocks assignments.

title: Auto-Tune LD Block Detection Parameters — tune_LD_params
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs; 0/1/2), genome-wide. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. gwas_df Data frame columns Marker, CHR, POS. grid Optional data frame parameter combinations. row one combination; columns must match parameter names Big_LD. NULL (default), sensible grid CLQcut (4 values) min_freq (2 values) used, giving 8 combinations. treated hyperparameters following Weber SE et al. (2023, Front. Plant Sci. 14:1217589, doi:10.3389/fpls.2023.1217589 ), show single threshold universally optimal across datasets traits. chromosomes Optional character vector chromosome names include tuning. NULL uses chromosomes snp_info. target_bp_band Length-2 numeric vector: preferred median block size range base pairs. Default c(5e4, 5e5) (50 kb - 500 kb). parallel Logical. TRUE, uses future.apply::future_lapply parallelism (user must set future plan calling). Default FALSE. seed Integer seed reproducibility. Default NULL. prefer_perfect Logical. Give priority parameter sets zero unassigned / zero forced GWAS markers. Default TRUE. return_all_perfect Logical. Include table zero-zero combinations returned list. Default TRUE.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: Louvain/Leiden community detection (CLQmode = "Louvain" "Leiden"): Polynomial-time O(n log n) community detection replaces Bron-Kerbosch (igraph::max_cliques()) exponential worst-case complexity. original Big-LD run 3M-SNP WGS panel found 4.26 million maximal cliques single 1500-SNP window ran > 1 hour without completing. Louvain/Leiden finish window < 1 second. Block boundaries equivalent better Density mode WGS panels LD structure dense. Available CLQD(), Big_LD(), run_Big_LD_all_chr(), tune_LD_params(), run_ldx_pipeline(). Edge weights inversely proportional base-pair distance local LD structure respected. Sparse LD computation (max_bp_distance parameter): max_bp_distance > 0, SNP pairs within physical distance r² computed via compute_r2_sparse_cpp(). Pairs beyond threshold assumed negligible LD set zero adjacency matrix. reduces O(p²) LD computation near-O(p) WGS panels — 500 kb, approximately 70–90% pairs skipped. Available CLQD(), propagated callers. Default 0L (disabled) preserves original behaviour. Memory-mapped genotype store (read_geno_bigmemory()): Wraps genotype source bigmemory::big.matrix backed binary file disk. read_chunk() retrieves columns via OS page faults — requested bytes loaded RAM. Peak RAM proportional n_samples × subSegmSize × 8 bytes, full genome matrix. Backing files persist across R sessions: supply .desc path reattach without re-loading source data. Storage type "char" (1 byte per cell) saves 8× RAM vs double 0/1/2 dosage values. Requires bigmemory (added Suggests).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_geno(): removed pheno_ids parameter. Sample subsetting caller’s responsibility – use $sample_ids match externally. Removed read_pheno() align_geno_pheno() entirely. Phenotype handling part LD block detection functions algorithmic role. run_Big_LD_all_chr(): parameter rV2method renamed kin_method. digits default changed 6 -1 (rounding) throughout. SeqArray backend replaced SNPRelate throughout. GDS files now created via SNPRelate::snpgdsVCF2GDS() read via SNPRelate::snpgdsGetGeno(). Existing .gds files created SeqArray compatible; delete re-convert read_geno(..., verbose = TRUE).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_phased_vcf(): read pre-phased VCF 0|1 GT fields; returns hap1/hap2 gamete matrices plus combined dosage. phase_with_beagle(): call Beagle 5.x statistical phasing WGS data; returns path phased VCF.gz. phase_with_pedigree(): Mendelian allele transmission phasing within parent-offspring trios; exact parents homozygous. unphase_to_dosage(): collapse phased gamete matrices back 0/1/2 (internal helper; exported). Auto-detects phased vs unphased input list structure. Unphased mode: diploid string "012201" per individual per block. Phased mode: two-gamete string "011|100" per individual per block. Blocks strictly per-chromosome; cross-chromosome blocks architecturally impossible. n_eff_alleles: effective number alleles = 1/Σpᵢ² (Hill 1973), ranging 1 (monomorphic) k (equal frequencies). sweep_flag: TRUE freq_dominant ≥ 0.90, flagging possible selective sweeps strong founder effects (Difabachew et al. 2023). now sample-size corrected following Nei (1973): = n/(n-1) × (1 − Σpᵢ²). Output now includes CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps columns direct use genomic region analyses. Converts raw dosage strings (e.g. "02110") nucleotide sequences (e.g. "AGTTA") using REF/ALT snp_info. Returns data frame: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, hap_rank, hap_id, dosage_string, nucleotide_sequence, frequency, n_carriers, snp_positions, snp_alleles. New encoding = parameter: "additive_012" (default) "presence_01". Phased + "additive_012": true 0/1/2 allele counts per gamete (0=absent, 1=one gamete, 2=gametes). Unphased + "additive_012": 0/1/NA — 1=present, 0=absent. value 2 used homozygosity inferred unphased strings. "presence_01": 0/1 presence/absence kernel methods random forests (formerly "presence_02"; "presence_02" accepted backward-compat alias). New min_freq = parameter drop rare haplotype allele columns. top_n default changed 5L NULL (retain alleles min_freq). Maps significant GWAS markers onto LD blocks post-GWAS. Flags pleiotropic blocks (significant hits multiple traits). Implements haploblock-based QTL cataloguing approach Tong et al. (2024) Theoretical Applied Genetics 137:274. Now accepts optional BETA column gwas_results. supplied, output includes: lead_beta (effect lead SNP), sig_snps (significant SNP IDs, semicolon-separated), sig_betas (marginal effects). See ?define_qtl_regions block effect estimation approaches. write_haplotype_numeric(): tab-delimited dosage matrix (0/1/2/NA). Metadata column alleles shows nucleotide sequence allele. Compatible rrBLUP, BGLR, ASReml-R. write_haplotype_character(): tab-delimited nucleotide matrix. cell shows nucleotide sequence individual carries allele, "-" absent, "." missing. Heterozygous SNP positions encoded IUPAC ambiguity codes (R=/G, Y=C/T, S=G/C, W=/T, K=G/T, M=/C), keeping sequence length n_snps. Alleles column shows REF/ALT every block SNP (semicolon-separated). write_haplotype_diversity(): CSV per-block diversity metrics optional genome-wide mean summary row. write_haplotype_hapmap() removed. diploid AA//TT HapMap encoding meaningful multi-SNP haplotype alleles. Haplotype string decoder (decode_haplotype_strings()): exported function mapping dosage strings nucleotide sequences full SNP-level metadata. Extends run_haplotype_prediction() k traits simultaneously. One trait-agnostic GRM (computed ) shared across traits. GBLUP solver: attempts sommer::mmer() multi-trait model first; automatically falls back rrBLUP::kin.blup() per-trait loop sommer installed model fails converge. Block importance aggregated across traits: var_scaled_mean, n_traits_important, important_any, important_all per block. importance_rule argument controls combined flag: '' (≥ 1 trait), '' (traits), 'mean' (mean ≥ 0.9). blues accepts wide data frame (one column per trait) named list named numeric vectors (different individuals per trait). sommer added Suggests; rrBLUP required fallback. run_haplotype_prediction_mt() removed; functionality fully absorbed unified run_haplotype_prediction() API. Cites Covarrubias-Pazaran (2016) sommer Endelman (2011) rrBLUP. compute_haplotype_grm(): VanRaden (2008) GRM haplotype feature matrix. Mean-imputes NA, clamps frequencies, returns symmetric n×n matrix. backsolve_snp_effects(): derives per-SNP additive effects GEBV without refitting marker model: α̂ = M′G⁻¹ĝ / 2Σp(1−p) (Tong et al. 2025 Theor Appl Genet 138:267). compute_local_gebv(): local haplotype GEBV per block = sum SNP effects within block. Ranks blocks Var(local GEBV); important flag scaled variance ≥ 0.90 (Tong et al. 2024 Theor Appl Genet 137:274). prepare_gblup_inputs(): aligns haplotype feature matrix phenotype data frame; computes returns bended GRM ready rrBLUP, sommer, ASReml-R, BGLR. run_haplotype_prediction(): end--end Tong et al. (2024/2025) pipeline pre-adjusted phenotype values (BLUEs/BLUPs) block importance. Accepts blues named numeric vector data frame id_col blue_col arguments. Uses rrBLUP::kin.blup() REML-based GBLUP. Returns standard pipeline outputs plus GEBV, per-SNP effects, local haplotype GEBV matrix, block importance table. integrate_gwas_haplotypes(): combines GWAS evidence (has_gwas_hit), variance evidence (is_important, scaled Var(local GEBV) ≥ 0.9), diversity evidence (is_diverse, ≥ threshold) priority_score (0–3) plain-language recommendation per block. diversity-— rank ; (2) GWAS-— binary GWAS-hit flag tiebreaker, p-value used ranking; (3) phenotype — rank scaled Var(local GEBV). Returns standard pipeline outputs plus ranked_blocks data frame. hap_format = "hapmap" renamed hap_format = "character". Big_LD arguments now exposed: clstgap, split, appendrare, singleton_as_block, checkLargest, digits, kin_method, CLQmode. Previously 8 silently using Big_LD defaults. min_freq parameter exposed (previously hardcoded inside build_haplotype_feature_matrix). Return list now includes geno_matrix (individuals × SNPs, MAF-filtered), enabling direct use tune_LD_params() run_haplotype_prediction() without reloading genotype file. tune_LD_params() default grid now jointly optimises CLQcut (4 values) min_freq (2 values), giving 8 combinations. Weber et al. (2023) show hyperparameters requiring dataset-specific tuning.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Multi-format /O: read_geno() supports numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF / bgzipped VCF, SNPRelate GDS, PLINK BED/BIM/FAM, plain R matrices. SNPRelate GDS streaming backend: peak RAM proportional one window. Replaces previous SeqArray backend entirely. PLINK BED backend: BEDMatrix-backed memory-mapped access. read_chunk(), close_backend(): unified accessor interface. run_Big_LD_all_chr() accepts LDxBlocks_backend directly. min_snps_chr parameter: skip scaffolds fewer SNPs (default 10). clean_malformed parameter read_geno(), run_Big_LD_all_chr(), run_ldx_pipeline(): streams input files remove malformed lines GDS conversion (NGSEP GATK-origin VCFs). chr parameter run_Big_LD_all_chr() run_ldx_pipeline(): restrict processing specific chromosomes. ldx_gwas example dataset now includes trait column ("TraitA" / "TraitB") demonstrate multi-trait pleiotropic block detection via define_qtl_regions(trait_col = "trait").

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: boundary_scan_cpp(): C++ boundary scan replaces R inner loop (~20x faster). maf_filter_cpp(): combined MAF + monomorphic filter, single O(np) pass (~10x faster panels > 100 k SNPs). build_adj_matrix_cpp(): -place adjacency construction, eliminates intermediate allocation. compute_r2_sparse_cpp(): sparse r² within bp window, avoids O(p²) cost large sub-segments.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Chunked pre-allocated numeric CSV reader: two-pass strategy – header scan Pass 1, fixed 50,000-row chunks fill single pre-allocated matrix Pass 2. Peak RAM = one chunk, 2x file. VCF HapMap mandatory SNPRelate GDS auto-conversion: transparent streaming GDS backend first call; cache reused subsequent calls. Explicit per-chromosome gc() run_Big_LD_all_chr() extract_haplotypes(): prevents heap fragmentation across 20-30 chromosome passes.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: test-basic.R: smoke tests covering major functions via 230-SNP example dataset (3 chromosomes, 9 LD blocks). test-algorithm.R: property-based tests Big-LD segmentation algorithm: CLQD, Big_LD, run_Big_LD_all_chr, summarise_blocks, plot_ld_blocks. test-haplotypes.R: comprehensive tests haplotype extraction (phased unphased), diversity metrics (n_eff_alleles, sweep_flag), QTL region definition (lead_beta, sig_snps, sig_betas), feature matrix encoding (additive_012 / presence_01), output writers, rank_haplotype_blocks, integrate_gwas_haplotypes.
